title: lithocarpus woodii
intro: Lithocarpus woodii, commonly known as the Himalayan Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which offer various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The therapeutic actions of Lithocarpus woodii include its use in treating respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Lithocarpus woodii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingestion of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Excessive use of the plant's extracts may lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue, as well as interact with other medications. Improper handling of the plant can also cause skin contact dermatitis and eye irritation.
